Mortgage FAQ

Ask the question you think you should already know the answer to.

You are not supposed to know all of this already. Search for a plain-language answer, see what depends on your actual situation, and reach out when the general answer is not enough.

Search the answersAsk Dennis directly

27 answers shown

Getting startedCan I explore my mortgage options without a credit check?

Yes. The pathfinder and calculators can organize your goals, payment assumptions, and likely questions without a credit pull. A formal preapproval is different: it normally requires your authorization, a credit review, and supporting information before a lender can make a credit decision.

Start the 60-second pathfinder →Open the calculators →
Getting startedWhat happens after I send Dennis my scenario?

Dennis reviews the goal, timing, rough payment range, and the questions that could change the path. You can then choose whether to keep planning, book a conversation, or move into the secure application. Starting the conversation does not obligate you to apply.

Build my starting plan →Book a conversation →
Getting startedDo you serve borrowers throughout Florida?

Dennis is licensed in Florida and works with buyers and homeowners statewide through a largely digital process. Property type, occupancy, program availability, and the details of the actual file still determine which options may be available.

Meet Dennis →
Getting startedHow is working with a mortgage broker different from working with a bank?

A bank generally offers its own lending menu. A mortgage broker can compare options from multiple wholesale lenders, including some specialty programs, but no channel is automatically best for every borrower. The useful comparison is the complete written offer: rate, APR, points, credits, fees, payment, cash to close, service, and program fit.

Ask Dennis to compare a scenario →
Payment & cashHow much cash might I actually need to buy a home?

Cash to close can include the down payment, lender and third-party closing costs, prepaid taxes and insurance, the initial escrow deposit, inspections, and sometimes reserves. The number changes with the loan, property, closing date, seller credits, and any eligible assistance, so it should be modeled as a complete scenario instead of guessed from the down-payment percentage alone.

Model a purchase payment →Explore DPA options →
Payment & cashIs the maximum preapproval the same as a comfortable home budget?

No. A preapproval is based on lending guidelines and the information reviewed; it does not know what you want left for childcare, travel, repairs, savings, or everyday life. A useful home budget starts with the complete payment and the cash you want to preserve, then works backward to a price range.

Open the affordability calculator →
Payment & cashWhat are closing costs?

Closing costs can include lender charges, appraisal, title and settlement services, recording charges, and other transaction-specific fees. Prepaid interest, taxes, insurance, and escrow funding also affect cash to close but are not all lender fees. A Loan Estimate tied to an actual application is the right document for comparing the details.

Request a scenario review →
Payment & cashCould down payment assistance help with my cash to close?

Possibly. Florida and local programs can depend on county, household income, household size, first-time-buyer definitions, occupation, credit, approved loan type, funding availability, education requirements, and purchase timing. Assistance also has repayment or forgiveness terms that should be evaluated with the first mortgage.

Check the details that shape DPA fit →
Payment & cashWhat is the difference between the interest rate and APR?

The interest rate is used to calculate loan interest. APR is a broader disclosure that incorporates the rate plus certain finance charges, expressed as an annual percentage. APR can help compare similar loan structures, but it does not replace reviewing points, credits, cash to close, projected payment, and how long you expect to keep the loan.

Get pricing tied to my scenario →
Payment & cashIf my mortgage rate is fixed, can my total monthly payment still change?

Yes. On a typical fixed-rate mortgage, scheduled principal and interest generally remain stable, but the complete housing payment can change when property taxes, homeowners or flood insurance, mortgage insurance, or escrow requirements change. HOA dues are another housing cost and are often paid separately.

Model the complete payment →
Loan programsHow should I compare FHA, VA, conventional, and USDA financing?

Compare eligibility, down payment, mortgage insurance or funding fees, rate and APR, property requirements, loan limits, cash to close, and the complete monthly payment. The lowest advertised rate or smallest down payment is not automatically the lowest-cost or best-fitting option for your timeline.

Compare mortgage paths →Run program-specific calculators →
Income & creditWhat credit score do I need for a mortgage?

There is no single website number that answers this reliably. Program rules, automated underwriting findings, lender overlays, credit history, debt-to-income ratio, reserves, property, occupancy, and loan purpose can all matter. Dennis can explain which paths may be worth reviewing without promising an approval from a score alone.

Organize my starting scenario →
Loan programsDoes FHA mortgage insurance always remain for the life of the loan?

Not always. For many newer FHA loans, annual mortgage-insurance duration depends on the original loan-to-value ratio and loan term. Some scenarios use an 11-year duration; higher original LTV scenarios can require annual MIP for the mortgage term. The actual case date and FHA rules should be verified before relying on a cancellation assumption.

Open the FHA calculator →
Loan programsWhat are the major VA loan benefits, and what is the funding fee?

Eligible VA borrowers may be able to purchase with no down payment and no monthly private mortgage insurance, subject to entitlement, approval, property, and program rules. Many VA loans include a one-time funding fee that can vary by loan purpose, down payment, and prior use; some borrowers are exempt. The complete payment and cash-to-close comparison still matters.

Run the VA purchase calculator →
Loan programsDoes a VA appraisal automatically make the loan close more slowly?

No blanket answer is accurate. VA appraisals have program-specific property and valuation requirements, while timing also depends on the market, appraiser assignment, property condition, lender workflow, title, insurance, documents, and underwriting. The contract timeline should be built around the actual property and local service expectations, not a stereotype about VA financing.

Discuss a VA offer strategy →
Loan programsWhat is a USDA loan and how is eligibility determined?

USDA-guaranteed financing can offer no-down-payment purchase options for eligible primary residences. Household income limits, property location, occupancy, credit and repayment ability, property standards, and current program rules all matter. A property that looks suburban may still be eligible, but location and income should be checked directly.

Ask Dennis to check the scenario →
Loan programsHow do I know whether refinancing is worth it?

Start with the goal: lower payment, shorter term, different risk, removing mortgage insurance, or accessing equity. Then compare the new loan amount, closing costs, payment change, interest over your expected holding period, and the time needed to recover costs. A lower rate by itself does not prove that a refinance helps.

Open the refinance calculator →
Income & creditCan I qualify for a mortgage if I am self-employed?

Yes, self-employed borrowers can have several possible paths. The useful review may include business history, ownership percentage, personal and business returns, year-to-date results, bank statements, liquidity, and how the income is documented. The right documentation is scenario-specific; not every borrower is automatically required to provide the same two-year package.

Review self-employed options →
Income & creditCan I buy a home while carrying student-loan debt?

Student-loan debt does not automatically prevent homeownership. The payment used in qualification can depend on the loan program, credit report, documented repayment terms, and current guidelines. The important step is modeling the qualifying payment together with every other monthly obligation.

Open the affordability calculator →
Income & creditWhat documents might be needed for a complete mortgage review?

Common requests include identification, income and employment records, asset statements, housing history, and documents explaining funds or unusual items. Self-employed, retired, commissioned, investment-property, military, and assistance-program files can require different evidence. Use the secure application for sensitive information. Never send Social Security numbers or account documents through a general contact form.

Open the secure application →
Income & creditWhen should I receive a Loan Estimate?

For a mortgage covered by the federal Loan Estimate rules, providing the six application items (name, income, Social Security number for the credit report, property address, estimated property value, and desired loan amount) generally triggers delivery or mailing of the Loan Estimate within three business days. A Loan Estimate is not an approval, and some products such as HELOCs use different disclosures.

Learn what belongs in the secure application →
Property & closingHow long does a mortgage closing take?

There is no reliable universal number. Contract dates, loan program, appraisal, title, insurance, condo or property review, assistance programs, document speed, underwriting conditions, and required disclosure periods can all affect the timeline. Dennis can help build a realistic schedule once the property and financing path are known.

Talk through a contract timeline →
Property & closingWhy should Florida homeowners insurance be checked early?

Insurance can materially change the monthly payment, cash to close, and sometimes property eligibility. Premium, wind coverage, flood requirements, deductibles, roof age, inspections, and carrier availability can all matter. A placeholder estimate is useful for planning, but it should be replaced with a property-specific quote before relying on the payment.

Model editable insurance →
Property & closingWhy can a Florida condo require more review than a single-family home?

The lender may need to review the unit and the condominium project, including insurance, budget, reserves, litigation, assessments, ownership concentration, structural or milestone information, and other program requirements. Checking the project early can prevent payment planning from getting ahead of property eligibility.

Ask about a condo before making assumptions →
Equity & investingHow should I compare a HELOC with a cash-out refinance?

A HELOC can preserve the existing first mortgage but usually introduces a variable-rate line and a separate payment. A cash-out refinance replaces the first mortgage and changes the rate, term, loan amount, and closing costs on the entire balance. Compare access, payment risk, total cost, timeline, and what happens if rates or balances change.

Open the HELOC calculator →Open the refinance calculator →
Equity & investingWhat does a DSCR calculator tell an investor?

Debt-service coverage ratio compares modeled property income with the debt payment or housing expense definition used by the program. Lenders can calculate qualifying rent and expenses differently, and requirements can include reserves, prepayment terms, appraisal-rent schedules, entity rules, and property limits. The calculator is a screening tool, not an approval.

Open the DSCR calculator →
Equity & investingWhat should a fix-and-flip financing model include?

A useful model includes purchase price, renovation budget, after-repair value, financed amount, points, interest, draw timing, closing costs, carrying costs, selling costs, contingency, and timeline risk. Small changes in sale price or project duration can change the result materially, so the model should be pressure-tested rather than treated as a forecast.

Open the Fix & Flip calculator →

Still unsure?

Bring Dennis the question you could not answer here.

Keep it high-level. Sensitive financial documents and personal identifiers belong only in the secure application.

Ask Dennis850-346-8514

Your next move

The FAQ can explain the general rule. Your situation gives it meaning.

If the answer still leaves you wondering what to do, send me the question and I will help you find the right next step.
